summary: No extra Maven dependencies or Docker service are needed. The artifact is the Maven SCM Git executable provider and depends only on normal transitive Maven SCM/Plexus/Commons libraries; meaningful end-to-end command coverage, if attempted, requires a local Git repository initialized by the test.
agent_guidance: Prefer updating the existing command-construction/consumer tests for the 1.11.1 API change. If exercising real provider commands rather than only static command builders, create a temporary local git repository or bare file:// remote in the test, run git init, configure user.name and user.email locally, and create an initial commit before invoking Maven SCM GitExe operations. Avoid network remotes.
risks: Tests that execute git depend on the git executable being available on PATH.
Real git command tests can be flaky unless they use only temp directories, local file:// remotes, and local repository config.
The compile failure shown is an API signature change in GitTagCommand.createCommandLine requiring the new boolean sign parameter, not a missing dependency or service.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #8274 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.maven.scm:maven-scm-provider-gitexe:1.11.1; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015
